title: Summer School on Edge AI 2025
content:
Summer School on Edge AI 2025

xiliu@helsinki.fi Tue, 26/05/2026 - 16:01

The Summer School on Edge AI 2025, hosted at KTH Royal Institute of Technology in Stockholm, Sweden, successfully brought together researchers, students, and industry representatives from across the Nordic region and beyond for four days of intensive learning, collaboration, and scientific exchange from September 1–4, 2025.

title: Farewell Celebration for Ritva Torppa
content:
Farewell Celebration for Ritva Torppa

txrissan@helsinki.fi Tue, 26/05/2026 - 10:12
On Wednesday, May 6, 2026, we celebrated the farewell of the long-time and well-liked university lecturer and researcher, docent Ritva Torppa. Ritva will retire from her position as a university lecturer over the summer.

ronkaine@helsinki.fi Mon, 25/05/2026 - 14:41

title: Patient-derived Heart Cells Offer New Insights into Severe Congenital Heart Disease
content:
Patient-derived Heart Cells Offer New Insights into Severe Congenital Heart Disease

eslotte@helsinki.fi Mon, 25/05/2026 - 13:00
A team of researchers from the University of Helsinki and the University of Turku has shown that heart muscle cells derived from
patients with hypoplastic left heart syndrome carry molecular vulnerabilities that may reduce their ability to withstand
developmental stress.

title: Helsingin yliopistolla kehitetty mittausmopo auttaa ymmärtämään kaupunkia katutason tarkkuudella
content:
A measurement e-scooter, developed at the University of Helsinki, yields street-level data on urban spaces

merimaaj@helsinki.fi Mon, 25/05/2026 - 17:07
A mobile measuring station produces street-level data on air quality, the weather, use of street space and the condition of the roads. Such data can help us understand the causes and effects of urban heat islands, among other things.

title: Miten luontokato pysäytetään tehokkaimmin?
content:
What are the most effective ways to stop biodiversity loss?

lindymar@helsinki.fi Fri, 22/05/2026 - 16:07
Professor of Conservation Biology Heini Kujala studies the effectiveness of different conservation methods and how to integrate information on biodiversity into everyday societal decision-making. Biodiversity loss threatens not only animals and plants but also human health and financial stability.

What are your research topics?

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.
